title: infront stack manifest (stack.yaml)
description: What a repo declares so infront can broker environments for it. Everything {{...}} is injected by the engine (symbolic ports, datastore URLs, service URLs). Policy (pool sizes, TTLs, substrates) never lives here.

name.description: Stack identity; pools are keyed by repo + this name.

services.description: Supervised shell commands, one per service. Commands, not containers.

caches.description: Paths (globs allowed) preserved by the bind-time reset: node_modules, **/obj, .angular, ...

keep.description: Generated files preserved across binds even though tracked/derived.

include.description: Git-ignored files that must ride along with a binding (.env.local).

outputs.description: Artifacts produced env-side but owned worktree-side (lockfiles, generated clients). Reported as outputs_changed; copied back only by explicit `infront pull`.

upkeep.description: Closed list of fingerprint->action rules, run at bind time in order (decision 0008).

logins.description: A seeded login consumers may use. Dev-grade by definition.

token.description: Command minting an auth token; {{role}} available. Must print JSON.

run.description: The supervised process. Daemon is its parent.

build.description: Run at bind when fingerprints demand; never during serve.

watch_run.description: Hot-reload alternative to run:, used only by --watch session leases.

cwd.description: Working directory relative to the repo root.

port.description: Symbolic port name; the engine allocates the number and injects {{ports.<name>}}. Omit for portless services (workers).

fatal_logs.description: ERE of log markers that fail a boot in seconds instead of polling readiness to timeout.

depends_on.description: Services that must be ready before this one starts.

http.description: Path polled on the service's port until 200.

log.description: ERE that must appear in the service log (portless services).

server.description: infront never runs datastore servers; it probes them.

probe.description: host:port TCP-probed before anything starts (server drivers). Failure is an infra-error, never code blame.

url.description: Connection-string template handed to services/checks; {{ns}} available. Required for server drivers; ignored for sqlite (the ns IS the file path).

create.description: Repo command that creates+seeds a namespace; {{ns}} and {{preset}} available.

drop.description: Repo command that drops a namespace; {{ns}} available. Best-effort (run before restore and on recycle).

template_restore.description: Repo command that clones a baked template namespace into a fresh one; {{template}} and {{ns}} available (e.g. postgres: createdb -T {{template}} {{ns}}). When present, `create` bakes once per seed-content hash and every bind restores in seconds.

template.description: sqlite only: bake a template file per seed-content hash and restore by copy. Server drivers use template_restore instead.

when.description: A file path or glob(...) whose content hash triggers the rule (direction-agnostic per-env ledger).

run.description: Repo command, or an engine built-in prefixed with @ (e.g. @rebake-template <datastore>).

artifacts.description: Globs collected into the verdict's artifacts dir.
